name: noble-solar
summary: The solar — a noble family's private upper chamber above the great hall. The warm, sunlit room where they actually live, away from court.

size_guidance.aspect_ratio: squarish, with big windows on one wall

primary_palette.note: ladies' solar — sewing and needlework by the window, main use of the room
family-hour solar — the lord and lady sit together, reading and talking
games and refreshment solar — cards or chess, private from the hall
musical solar — the lady plays for her household, small audience seating
the lady's study — a private desk in the family room, correspondence central

story_prompts: the lady sits at her frame — afternoon light, everyone else is elsewhere, this is her hour
a family gathering — the children on the rug, the parents by the fire, a rare unguarded moment
a secret meeting — the solar is private in a way no other room is; plots ferment here
the solar in summer — windows open, embroidery abandoned, the family in the garden below
the widowed lady has moved her whole life into the solar — bed rolled in, she will not leave

lighting_notes.ambient_intent: bright warm (0.55-0.7) — the solar is named for sun, let light in

anti_patterns: the solar is SUNNY — big windows are the defining feature, even if not prop-placeable
differentiate from the bedchamber — no bed dominant, no garderobe, activities matter
this is the family's real room — comfortable, slightly cluttered with hobbies, lived-in
not a formal reception room — no symmetry demands, no courtiers, no throne
